Amy Gooding, CSR#13386 408-828-0989 agreporting@outlook.com Court reporter

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KENNETH WILLIAMS' Motion For Relief From Orders Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted And Orders Granting Motions For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Plaintiff Kenneth Williams' motion for relief from orders deeming requests for admissions admitted and orders granting motions for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The sole grounds for this motion is the asserted misconduct of the A1 Law Group constitutes extrinsic fraud. However, the record shows that plaintiff was aware of and had an opportunity to oppose the motions which he seeks to have set aside and thus any fraud perpetrated against him is intrinsic fraud which is not a basis to set aside otherwise valid court orders.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Notice Of Motion To Have Request For Admissions, Set One Deemed Admitted, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Angela Jackson, power of attorney for Plaintiff Kenneth Williams.
Atty. Tom Prountzos, Esq. of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP (tel: 415-705-0400) for Defendants Faith First Bancorp, Inc. and Elizabeth Marithza Poe-Abad.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS ELIZABETH POE-ABAD, AND FAITH FIRST BANCORP, INC.'s Motion To Have Request For Admissions, Set One Deemed Admitted, And Request For Sanctions.

Matter discussed. Angela Jackson states Plaintiff Kenneth Williams is not present with her. The Court states it cannot hear argument from Angela Jackson, as the Plaintiff is not present. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Moving party Elizabeth Poe-Abad's and Faith First Bancorp's Inc. motion for a deemed-admitted order is granted. The court grants the sanctions request in part.

The court exercises its discretion to consider plaintiff Kenneth Williams's late-filed opposition.

Moving parties seek an order that the matters specified in their requests for admission, served on June 4, 2025, be deemed admitted because Williams has not yet responded. Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(c) requires the court to enter this order. Williams's opposition recites that he does not have counsel and would like more time to complete discovery, but acknowledges that he has not responded to this discovery. Moving parties are therefore entitled to the deemed-admitted order they seek as a matter of law.

Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(c) further requires the court to impose a monetary sanction on Williams. Again, this relief is mandatory: the statute says, "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court in the exercise of its discretion in light of all the circumstances sets the sanction at $50. Williams shall pay this amount to Poe-Abad and Faith First Bancorp within 30 days of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
